etymology.description: 言(言) (yán, "word") with 甘(甘) (gān, "sweet") -> a depiction of sound emanating from the mouth -> 甘 stylized as 曰(曰) (yuē) -> final form 音
etymology.interpretation: The character depicts a word or sound (言) coming from the mouth, initially represented by 甘 as the body of the tongue, which transforms into 曰. This evolution symbolizes the emergence of sound from the mouth, capturing the essence of 'sound' and 'tone.'

examples.chinese: 我最喜欢听大提琴的声音(我最喜歡聽大提琴的聲音)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zuì xǐhuān tīng dàtíqín de shēngyīn.
examples.english: I love the sound of the cello the most.

examples.chinese: 她的声音很悦耳(她的聲音很悅耳)。
音乐可以抚慰人心(音樂可以撫慰人心)，给生活增添色彩(給生活增添色彩)。
examples.pinyin: Yīnyuè kěyǐ fǔwèi rénxīn, gěi shēnghuó zēngtiān sècǎi.
examples.english: Music can soothe the soul and add color to life.
